subject: [PATCH v2 0/2] mm/vmpressure: reduce CPU, memory and code overhead on cgroup v2

reviews.author: Michal Koutný
reviews.summary: Reviewer Michal Koutný questioned the statement that tree=false cannot be removed, pointing out that patch 1 already bails out from vmpressure() when tree=true, and asked for clarification on why this path cannot be eliminated.
reviews.raw_body:
(Here I understand PSI is a different and differntly scope metric) but
what does it mean when you write that tree=false cannot be removed but
the other patch bails out from vmpressure() (i.e. nothing is updated
anyway)?

Thanks,
Michal

reviews.author: Usama Arif (author)
reviews.summary: The author clarified the distinction between tree=true and tree=false paths in vmpressure, explaining that the first patch only optimizes for cgroup v2 when tree=true, while still using the original path for networking socket-buffer back-off when tree=false. The author acknowledged this as a separate scope of work from switching to PSI.
reviews.raw_body:
So the first patch bails out for cgroup v2 for tree = true only.
For tree = false, it doesn't bail out, and is still used for networking
socket-buffer back-off. I think that is a whole another scope of work
switching to PSI. Hope that makes sense?

Thanks,
Usama

reviews.author: Michal Koutný
reviews.summary: Reviewer Michal Koutný pointed out that patch 2/2 will have an unintended effect on the behavior of tree=true code, as it will not be compiled in for cgroup v2 and thus change more behavior than intended.
reviews.raw_body:
I've mixed mutliple things together, sorry. I wanted to actually ask
about your response:

| I realized when trying to swap the order that the splitting off v1
| commit will end up doing more that what I think it should do (just
| splitting off v1 specific code), as the tree = true code will not get
| compiled in at all for cgroup v2, and it then ends up changing more
| behaviour.

tree=true won't get compiled but v2 doesn't care about it, so the effect
of patch 2/2 should still be same (regardless whether it comes 1st or
2nd).
Do you refer to the invocation of vmpressure_v1_account_tree() that is
affected by this?

Thanks,
Michal

reviews.author: Usama Arif (author)
reviews.summary: Author clarified that they want to keep the optimization of not running vmpressure for tree=true + cgroup v2 as a separate change, not bundled with the patch that splits off v1 code.
reviews.raw_body:
So what I mean is that I want to keep the effect of the patch that splits
off v1 code as just that and not have the optimization of not running
vmpressure for tree = true + cgroup v2.

vmpressure_v1_account_tree() compiles to an empty function in the split
patch for cgroup v2, so if we make the splitting out v1 code as the first
patch, the commit is not just going to split v1 code but also do the
optimization of not running tree = true cgroup v2.

I hope it makes sense?

reviews.author: Johannes Weiner
reviews.summary:
Gave Acked-by

Reviewer Johannes Weiner noted that the patch's early return in vmpressure() for cgroup v2 + tree=true case is not sufficient, as it still calls try_to_unmap() while holding the per-vswap spinlock, which can lead to a lock ordering violation with reclaim paths.

reviews.author: Johannes Weiner
reviews.summary: Reviewer suggested moving the vmpressure interface code from mm/vmpressure-v1.c to memcontrol-v1.c, indicating that this change would be a better design choice.
reviews.raw_body:
Might as well move the interface part to memcontrol-v1.c instead of
creating a new file.

Otherwise looks good to me.

reviews.author: Michal Koutný
reviews.summary: Reviewer Michal Koutný expressed understanding of the patch's optimizations and cleanup after initial confusion.
reviews.raw_body:
All clear now, it's clicked for me what is the optimization and what the
cleanup.

Thanks,
Michal

reviews.author: Usama Arif (author)
reviews.summary: Author addressed Johannes Weiner's concern about the future of vmpressure-v1.c by suggesting two possible solutions: renaming it to just vmpressure.c and gating it to CONFIG_MEMCG_V1, or moving everything to memcontrol-v1.c. Author prefers keeping it separate as a standalone feature.
reviews.raw_body:
I think it would make it easier once we move cgroup v2 off of
vmpressure. Then we can rename vmpressure-v1.c to just vmpressure.c
and gate it to CONFIG_MEMCG_V1. The other option would be everything
living in memcontrol-v1.c? I think its nice to keep it separate as
memcontrol-v1.c is already 2K+ lines and this is standalone feature
that can sit in a separate file.

reviews.author: Johannes Weiner
reviews.summary:
Reviewer suggested moving code from mm/vmpressure-v1.c to memcontrol-v1.c, effectively merging the two files

The reviewer suggested moving deprecated code from mm/vmpressure.c into a single contiguous block under memcg1_oom_finish() to simplify maintenance and make it easier to track the footprint of cgroup v1, prioritizing this over maintaining neat component separation.
reviews.raw_body:
Hm? I just mean move whatever you move into that new vmpressure-v1.c
into memcontrol-v1.c instead.

---

It's all deprecated code with no active development. Containing it and
making it easy to see the footprint of the cgroup1 option is the
higher priority, more than maintaining neat component separation.

No need to work it naturally into the file either - decls/variables up
top, functions somewhere further down etc. Just paste it into one
contiguous vmpressure block under memcg1_oom_finish(). That's how that
file is already structured with the other components in there.

2026-06-29.patch_summary: This patch reduces CPU, memory, and code overhead in the cgroup v2 version of the vmpressure subsystem by eliminating unnecessary work when tree=true. It does this by extending an existing early return to skip the full lock/accumulate/schedule_work/parent-walk dance when tree=true on a cgroup v2 host. Additionally, it splits the v1 userspace eventfd interface into its own file (mm/vmpressure-v1.c) and removes unnecessary code from mm/vmpressure.c, resulting in significant memory savings. This is a step towards eventually making vmpressure CONFIG_MEMCG_V1 only.
